content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Members of the Commission

Date: June 14, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Ted Prim

Subject: Regulation §18737

On June 12, 1978, the Fair Political Practices Commission approved Regulation 18737. This regulation permits a code reviewing body to temporarily suspend certain provisions of a Conflict of Interest Code in order to protect the rights of a designated employee who is appealing to his code reviewing body regarding his designation in the agency's Conflict of Interest Code.

At the time it adopted this regulation, the Commission delegated its powers under this regulation to the Conflict of Interest Committee for the period between Commission meetings. Any suspension granted by the Conflict of Interest Committee automatically lapses at the end of the next Commission meeting unless the suspension is ratified by the Commission.

TP:mfa

sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal memorandum, not an advice letter or response to an incoming request. No question or conclusion sections present. Structured as an administrative notice of regulatory approval and delegation of authority.

This regulation permits a code reviewing body to temporarily suspend certain provisions of a Conflict of Interest Code in order to protect the rights of a designated employee who is appealing to his code reviewing body regarding his designation in the agency's Conflict of Interest Code. embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum to Commission members dated June 14, 1978, announcing the approval of Regulation §18737, which allows code reviewing bodies to temporarily suspend certain Conflict of Interest Code provisions to protect employee rights during designation appeals. The memo notes that enforcement powers under this regulation were delegated to the Conflict of Interest Committee between Commission meetings, with suspensions automatically lapsing at the end of the next Commission meeting unless ratified.
